one_line_thesis: Aehr Test Systems sells wafer-level burn-in systems into SiC and memory cycles; at $77.36 the proof-first component value ($9.79/sh base) implies -33.31% annual return on mid-cycle owner cash — watch until shipment recovery is filing-confirmed.

deep_dive.executive_summary: At $77.36 (Yahoo close 2026-07-20), our base case annual return is -33.31% per year over seven years on mid-cycle normalized owner cash $0.16 per share. Aehr is a cyclical burn-in equipment supplier with FOX-XP/WaferPak specialization. FY2025 marked a post-boom trough: revenue $59 million, gross margin near 41%, net loss $3.9 million, and free cash burn $12.4 million ($0.42 per share). The balance sheet carries $36.9 million cash and no term debt. The proof-first component schedule sums to $9.79 per share base economic value. Moat is stable in a focused niche; dhando is partial given cash p...

decision.next_action: Close critical evidence gaps before freezing a decision-grade packet.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Power Zones select the fitting economic method and eligible independent reviewers; they never choose the valuation answer or capital decision.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

timeline.label: Operating Cash Flow moved +321.4% versus the prior period (1,756.0 to 7,400.0).
Filed $60M at-the-market equity distribution agreement; optional dilution overhang at rich valuation
Q3 FY2026 revenue $10.3M (down YoY); nine-month net income $8.5M; cash $36.9M
Annual meeting approved 2.5M-share equity plan increase and ESPP expansion
FY2025 trough: revenue $59.0M, net loss $3.9M, operating cash outflow $7.4M after SiC order pause
FY2024 cycle peak net income $33.2M on $66.2M revenue; SiC burn-in demand elevated
SiC wafer-level burn-in ramp drove revenue to $65.0M from $20.3M two years prior
Pre-boom base revenue $20.3M before SiC capacity build-out cycle

industry.structure: Semiconductor burn-in and test is a niche within wafer-fabrication equipment; Aehr competes with custom in-house test and broader ATE vendors on FOX-XP and WaferPak systems. Gross margins near 40% at scale but orders are lumpy.
industry.share_shift: Aehr gained share during the 2023–2024 SiC burn-in upcycle but FY2025 revenue fell 11% as a major customer paused capacity adds.
industry.trend: SiC power-device and AI accelerator burn-in remain structural growth narratives, while customer concentration and order timing drive earnings volatility. Deferred revenue and install-base upgrades partially offset system shipment gaps.
